title: Helenium ammophilum
intro: Helenium ammophilum, also known as Sea Holly or Beach Sunflower, is a perennial plant species native to the coastal regions of Europe and North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive, prickly foliage. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Helenium ammophilum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its pharmacological activities. Helenium ammophilum is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as topical creams and salves, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Helenium ammophilum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to the southwestern United States. It is a perennial plant that typically grows to be around 1-2 feet tall and produces clusters of small, yellow flowers. The genus Helenium is a group of flowering plants commonly known as sneezeweed, which are native to North America. Helenium ammophilum is often found growing in sandy or dry areas, such as beaches or desert washes. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Helenium ammophilum is closely related to other plants like daisies and sunflowers.
uses: Helenium ammophilum, also known as Seaside Sunflower, has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots and leaves have been employed in folk remedies to treat various ailments, including skin irritations and wounds. It is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which may help in preventing infections and promoting wound healing. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Helenium ammophilum in medicinal applications. The plant's extracts may also have antioxidant properties, contributing to its potential health benefits.

constituents: Helenium ammophilum contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes as its active constituents. These compounds have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The flavonoids present in Helenium ammophilum may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. Further research is needed to fully understand the effects of these compounds on human health.
